summarization.a: A spectra chart displaying XPS intensity against binding energy in the 698-682 eV region, with two labeled curves representing different concentrations of CF4.
summarization.b: A spectra chart displaying XPS intensity against binding energy in the 298-292 eV region, with multiple labeled peaks indicating different chemical bonds.

a.question: At what binding energy does the F(1s) peak appear in both deposited films?

b.question: How does the carbon bonding environment in the deposited fluorocarbon films change as the CF₄ plasma fraction increases from 3.3% to 6.6%?
b.answer: The C(1s) spectra reveal that higher CF₄ fractions produce more heavily fluorinated polymer films. At 3.3% CF₄, the film shows predominantly lower-fluorination states (C-CFn peak around 285 eV) with modest C-F, C-F₂, and C-F₃ contributions. At 6.6% CF₄, the C-Fx peaks (C-F, C-F₂, C-F₃) become much more pronounced, indicating higher concentrations of carbon atoms bonded to multiple fluorine atoms.

b.question: Are C-F₃ bonds present in films deposited at both CF₄ fractions?
What carbon-fluorine bonding states are identified in the C(1s) spectra from low to high binding energy?
